title: Unlimited Tools MCP upstream config (E07 security model)
description: DESIGN (E07): upstream list for the Unlimited Tools gateway under the upstream security model (docs/mcp-upstream-security-model.md). Upstreams are stdio subprocesses only; every upstream carries a trust level; environment forwarding is allowlist-of-names only (never wildcards, never literal values); size and timeout limits are bounded and exceeding them is a structured refusal, never silent truncation. Trust-level ceilings (e.g. local-restricted max_response_bytes <= 1048576) are enforced semantically by the gateway at config load time; this schema enforces the hard bounds.

startup_timeout_seconds.description: Default per-upstream spawn+initialize deadline in seconds. Overridable per upstream within the same hard bound.

request_timeout_seconds.description: Default per-upstream request deadline in seconds. Overridable per upstream within the same hard bound. On timeout the upstream is terminated and the call is refused with JSON-RPC error -32002.

audit_max_bytes.description: Rotate the JSONL audit log when the active file exceeds this many bytes.

audit_max_files.description: Number of rotated audit log generations to keep.

name.description: Unique upstream id. Tools are addressed as '<name>.<tool>'.

enabled.description: false forces 'disabled' semantics regardless of trust_level: never spawned, never indexed, addressed calls refused with upstream_disabled (-32005).

trust_level.description: Trust level governing command form, environment forwarding, and size ceilings. 'future-remote-placeholder' is reserved: any operation on it is refused with trust_level_violation (-32010) until the OAuth/remote gate opens.

command.description: Executable to spawn, passed as argv[0] with no shell interpretation. Must be an absolute path at local-restricted; may be a known-runner bare name (node, npx, bunx, deno, python, python3, uv, uvx) at local-trusted. Violations are refused with command_not_allowed (-32006).

args.description: Command-line arguments, passed verbatim as an argv vector. No shell expansion, no %VAR%/$VAR templating.

cwd.description: Absolute path to an existing working directory for the upstream process. Default: a gateway-managed per-upstream scratch directory (the gateway's own cwd is never inherited). Relative paths are refused at config load time.

env_allowlist.description: Names of environment variables copied from the gateway's environment into the upstream process, in addition to the fixed minimal base set. Names only — never values, never wildcards (the item pattern makes '*' unrepresentable). Empty or absent forwards nothing beyond the base set. Broader forwarding is refused with env_forwarding_denied (-32007). Values are never written to any log.

startup_timeout_seconds.description: Spawn+initialize deadline override for this upstream, in seconds, bounded.

request_timeout_seconds.description: Per-request deadline override for this upstream, in seconds, bounded.

max_schema_bytes.description: Cap on one tool's serialized inputSchema as returned by tools_schema. Exceeding it is refused with schema_too_large (-32008) — never truncated. Trust-level ceiling at local-restricted: 262144.

max_response_bytes.description: Cap on one serialized tools/call result before relay. Exceeding it is refused with response_too_large (-32009) — the result is dropped, never trimmed. Trust-level ceiling at local-restricted: 1048576.

audit_level.description: minimal: ts/tool/upstream/duration_ms/ok only. standard: minimal plus redacted args shape and path-scrubbed error. There is deliberately no 'off'. Env values, tool results, prompts, skill bodies, raw schemas, and unscrubbed paths are never written at any level.

tools.description: Optional pre-declared tool index (names and descriptions only) so tools_search can match this upstream before it is ever spawned. Ignored for disabled and future-remote-placeholder upstreams.
